name: Oatmeal Cookies (Low Fat)
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg equivalent (commercial replacer, two whites or 1/4 cup "egg beaters")
1 Cup cake flour (all-purpose is okay but not as tender)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Line two baking sheets with parchment or coat with non-stick spray.
3. Toast oats: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add half the oats. Toast, stirring frequently until the oats begin to color and become fragrant (about 15 minutes). Repeat with remaining oats.
4. In a large bowl, whisk together sugar, applesauce, egg equivalent, and vanilla extract. Stir in raisins.
5. In a medium bowl, stir together dry ingredients: cake flour, whole wheat flour, baking soda, ground cinnamon, and salt.
6. Add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture and mix just until blended.
7. Drop dough by tablespoonfuls onto prepared baking sheets, spacing cookies about 4 cm apart.
8. Bake for 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Transfer cookies to a wire rack to cool.
Storage: Low-fat-added cookies do not store well. They become stale quickly and stick together if stacked. As soon as they are cool, wrap the cookies in layers separated by waxed paper and freeze. To serve, simply let them thaw to room temperature.
